_comment: SILLYTAVERN LOREBOOK TEMPLATE v3.0 | Based on Angela, Chloe, Megan cards
name: CHARNAME

entries.content: [PLACEHOLDER] Describe the setting: year, country, city, technology level, cultural context, and whether magic/fantasy elements exist. Example: 'The year is 2030, New York City. Advanced 21st-century technology, no magic.' This entry is CONSTANT (always active), position=after_char, depth=1 (system level). No trigger keys needed.

[PLACEHOLDER] List all important NPCs with brief descriptions. Format: Important_NPCs("Name: role and personality", "Name: role and personality"). This entry is CONSTANT (always active), position=after_char, depth=1. No trigger keys. Order=90 inserts before the WORLD entry. Create one such entry covering all major NPCs.

[PLACEHOLDER] Describe the main building or location layout. Format: Place_Layout("Area: description", "Area: description"). Example rooms: living room, kitchen, bedroom, bathroom. This entry is CONSTANT, position=before_char (inserted BEFORE character card), depth=4 (chat history level). Order=70. No trigger keys.

[PLACEHOLDER] Detailed description of a specific NPC. Include: appearance, personality, relationship to {{char}}, and role in the story. This entry is NORMAL (not constant) - it only fires when the NPC's name appears in chat. Add one entry per important NPC. constant=false, position=after_char, depth=4. Order=140. Keys: the NPC's name (and translation if applicable).

[PLACEHOLDER] Character backstory and history. Include: childhood events, formative experiences, trauma, education, past relationships. This entry is NORMAL - fires only when backstory-related keywords appear in chat. constant=false, position=after_char, depth=4. Order=150. Keys: use regex patterns matching keywords like 'past', 'childhood', 'school', 'trauma', 'history', 'backstory' (include both English and non-English variants). Set use_regex=true if using regex keys.

[PLACEHOLDER] Family members and their relationships with {{char}}. Include: mother, father, siblings, extended family. Describe each member's personality, relationship dynamics, and any family history. This entry is NORMAL - fires when family-related keywords appear. constant=false, position=after_char, depth=4. Order=160. Keys: regex patterns matching 'mother', 'father', 'parent', 'family', 'mom', 'dad', and non-English equivalents. Set use_regex=true.
